title: dactylorhiza urvilleana var. pontica
intro: Dactylorhiza urvilleana var. pontica, also known as Pontic Autumn Lady's-tresses, is a rare and endangered variety of orchid that is native to the Pontic Mountains in Eastern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza urvilleana var. pontica include its ability to help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in the management of diabetes and cardiovascular disease. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
